title: Decision intake
heading: Before we decide
subhead: A kitchen-sink intake exercising every primitive the skill ships. Theme deliberately departs from the default to avoid reinforcing the cool-blue SaaS reflex.

questions.label: If it goes badly, how hard is it to undo?
questions.inferenceBox: Most engineering decisions are more reversible than they feel in the moment. Be honest about which bucket this lives in.

options.label: Moderately reversible
options.description: Costs hours to days to undo.

options.label: Hard to reverse
options.description: Compounds quickly; undoing is a project.

questions.label: How confident are you in your own read of this decision?
In one sentence, what is the actual question?
questions.placeholder: e.g., Should we adopt Postgres for the analytics path?

questions.title: Now for the stakes.
questions.body: Understanding what's on the line — and how you weight competing concerns — shapes the recommendation you'll get back.

questions.label: Order the decision factors from most to least weight
questions.hint: Drag rows or use the ↑↓ buttons. Top position gets the most weight on your final call.

options.label: Expected impact
options.description: How much does this move the needle?

options.label: Downside risk
options.description: How bad is the worst case?

options.label: Time to execute
options.description: How long before you see results?

options.label: Reversibility
options.description: Can you undo it if you're wrong?

questions.label: How will this decision be made?
How much capital is at risk?
Which constraints actually bind?
questions.hint: Pick what is genuinely non-negotiable, not aspirational.

questions.label: Will you decide alone, or with others?

options.label: With others
options.description: Buy-in needed before this can land.

questions.label: Any unmovable date or external trigger?
Attach any supporting document (optional)
questions.hint: Briefing notes, prior analysis, or relevant data. Embedded in the export.

knownContext: User: a senior engineer on a small product team.
Decision archive: prior decisions are logged in the team's decision record.

routing.ask.reversibility: route to a structured decision workflow with reversibility as the primary axis when 'hard'
routing.ask.deciders: loop in the wider group when buy-in needs to be broad
